data.title: Perma-Pipe International appoints Saleh Sagr as president, CEO
data.description:
• Perma-Pipe International (NASDAQ:PPIH) has appointed Saleh Sagr as its president and CEO, following the departure of David Mansfield, effective June 7, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Hapbee upgrades US listing to OTCID to expand investor access, increase market visibility
data.description:
• Hapbee Technologies (TSXV:HAPB:CA) said on Thursday that its U.S. listing has been upgraded from the OTC Pink Sheets to the newly established OTCID (OTC International Designated) tier on the OTC Markets Group platform.
  • The company will continue to trade under the symbol HAPBF once the upgrade is complete, effective July 1, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Merck Animal Health gets EU backing for Numelvi tablets
data.description:
• Merck Animal Health, a unit of Merck & Co (NYSE:MRK) said that the European Medicines Agency’s Committee for Veterinary Medicinal Products (CVMP) recommended the approval of Numelvi tablets for dogs.
  • Based on the CVMP’s recommendation, the European Commission is expected to issue a decision for marketing authorization during the third quarter of 2025.

data.title: Flowco authorizes $50M stock repurchase program
data.description: Flowco Holdings (NYSE:FLOC) announced a $50M stock repurchase program. The buyback targets the Company’s Class A common stock. Source: Press release

data.title: RH beats Q1 expectations, keeps guidance unchanged despite tariff uncertainties
data.description:
Shares of RH (NYSE:RH) are rallying in Thursday’s after-hours trading as the company’s efforts to increase investments in Europe, expand and elevate its product offerings, and mitigate tariff headwinds delivered better-than-expected results for the first quarter.

For the quarter ended May 3, the luxury home furnishings' retailer earned an adjusted profit of $0.13 per share. This compares to a loss of $0.40 in the same quarter last year and beat expectations for a loss of $0.07. And despite operating in the “worst housing market in 50 years,” sales were up 12% to an above-consensus $814M.

Adjusted operating margin improved by 50 basis points to 7% while adjusted EBITDA margin increased 80 basis points to 13.1%, both of which were at the high end of the company’s expectations.

Looking ahead to the current quarter and fiscal year 2025, RH (NYSE:RH) continues to take steps to mitigate the impact of U.S. import tariff with a goal to shift sourcing from China to just 2% from 16%, currently, with a “meaningful portion” of costs to be absorbed by vendors.

The company will also delay the launch of the new concept planned for the second half of 2025 to the spring of 2026 when there is more certainty regarding tariffs.

Additionally, due to the “significant and unexpected” Liberation Day tariffs announced on April 2, the disruption in shipments and resourcing efforts will negatively impact revenues by ~6 points in Q2 and will be recovered in the second half of the year.  

For the second quarter, revenue is expected to increase by 8% to 10%, translating into Q2 revenue of $896.03M to $912.63M, below the $915.8M estimate. Adjusted operating margin is pegged for 15% to 16% and adjusted operating margin 15% to 16%.

The company expects FY25 revenue growth of 10% to 13%, translating to $3.5B to $3.6B versus $3.52B estimates. Adjusted operating margin is expected to be between 14% to 15% and adjusted EBITDA margin of 20% to 21%. Free cash flow is targeted to be between $250M to $350M.

“While there remains uncertainty until the reciprocal tariff negotiations are complete, we have proven we are well positioned to compete favorably in any market conditions,” the company said in a statement.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Nano Dimension's Q1 loss decreases 33%
Forum Energy Technologies to supply submarine rescue system for Indonesia Navy
data.description:
Forum Energy Technologies (NYSE:FET) said Thursday it was awarded a contract to supply a state-of-the-art submarine rescue vehicle system for use by Indonesia's Navy; financial details were not disclosed.

The new LR600 model SRV, a piloted rescue submersible, will be capable of operating at depths of up to 600 meters, carrying 20 people, and will be fully integrated with a hyperbaric rescue facility while also utilizing the latest technologies to locate a distressed submarine and quickly transfer personnel, the company said, adding that sea trials are planned for 2028.

The SRV will be built at Forum Energy's (FET) facility in Kirkbymoorside, Yorkshire, England, and the bespoke LARS will be manufactured at the company's Bryan, Texas, facility.
